title: lupinus argenteus var. montigenus
intro: Lupinus argenteus var. montigenus, commonly known as the montane silvery lupine, is a perennial legume plant that belongs to the lupine family. It is native to the mountainous regions of North America and is characterized by its silvery-gray foliage and tall spikes of blue-purple flowers. The medicinal properties of this plant include its ability to treat skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory issues due to its high content of alkaloids, particularly lupanine. Lupine root preparations, typically made from the dried root, have been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and inflammation, while also providing antioxidant and anti-inflammatory benefits.
